assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation). Overall eval pass rate: 91% (54/56 assertions across 5 evals).' The Test Quality verdict is explicitly WARN, driven by the Eval Quality WARN component.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 36-39: 'An eval result review was detected from github-actions[bot] (review ID 40001) using the 3-criteria heuristic: 1. Author is github-actions[bot] -- matched, 2. Body contains ## Eval Results marker -- matched, 3. Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer -- matched.' The Test Quality row (line 13) reflects extracted eval metrics: pass rates per eval and failing assertion details.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 11: '## Eval Result Detection: NOT an eval result' -- explicitly not an eval result. Line 19: Classification is 'Code Change Request' through standard classification pipeline with language analysis, review state, gap identification, and actionable request reasoning. No eval metrics referenced in the classification.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes: 'Overall eval pass rate: 91% (54/56 assertions across 5 evals)' and per-eval breakdown 'eval-3: 11/13 (85%)'. Failing assertions detailed: '(1) convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for review comment 30002; (2) no sub-task created for review comment 30002 -- suggestion not elevated to code change request via convention analysis.'

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and targets eval-3 failures. Line 8: 'Two assertions are failing in eval-3 (85% pass rate, 2 failures out of 13 assertions)'. The file includes both failing assertion texts and evidence in the Review Context section (lines 31-37).

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 31-37 under '## Review Context': Quotes both assertions with full text and evidence. Assertion 1 about convention upgrade eligibility with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup. Assertion 2 about sub-task creation with evidence about no convention upgrade being attempted.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 39-40: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747' -- exact URL match.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE |' -- verdict is DONE, not N/A. Lines 66-80 provide full root-cause investigation including universality test (Universal), method-vs-fact test (Method), classification (Skill gap, implement-task phase), and detailed skill phase investigation with sections (a), (b), (c).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 output files are verification/reporting artifacts: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. No source code files appear in the outputs directory. The outputs consist exclusively of read-only analysis, classification, and reporting documents.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: Scope Containment (line 7) independently evaluates 'Changes are scoped to the two files specified in the task' with no eval references. Sensitive Patterns (line 10) independently reports 'No secrets, credentials, API keys, tokens, or sensitive data patterns detected'. Acceptance Criteria (line 12) independently evaluates '7 of 7 criteria met' with specific criterion-by-criterion checks. Each operates on diff content independently of eval processing.
